sections.title: The passive (continuous, perfect, past perfect, be going to, modals)

content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: be in the correct tense + past participle

content.subtitle: Tense transformations (active → passive)
rows: present continuous
My aunt is doing the washing-up.
The washing-up **is being done** by my aunt.
present perfect simple
My cousin has sent the invitations.
The invitations **have been sent** by my cousin.
past continuous
My uncle was cleaning the car.
The car **was being cleaned** by my uncle.
past perfect simple
Our neighbours had taken the twins to the zoo.
The twins **had been taken** to the zoo by our neighbours.
be going to
They're going to invite Phil to the party.
Phil **is going to be invited** to the party.
modal (might)
They might invite Kyle to the party.
Kyle **might be invited** to the party.
modal (should)
We should tell Jenny about the party.
Jenny **should be told** about the party.
modal (must)
We must tell Dominic about the concert.
Dominic **must be told** about the concert.
modal (can)
We can hold the party at Jack's house.
The party **can be held** at Jack's house.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Use **by** to introduce the person or thing that performs the action: The garden was designed **by** a famous architect.
Use **with** to introduce the tool or instrument used: The wood was cut **with** a special saw.
Leave out **by** or **with** when the agent or instrument is unknown or not relevant: The patient was taken to hospital immediately.

sections.title: A: Original course practice
sections.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.

items.sentence: During the class project on The passive (2), Mia wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: The washing-up is being done by my aunt.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about The passive (2), the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: The invitations have been sent by my cousin.

items.sentence: Sara checked the course practice notes and added ...... to the example.
items.answer: The twins had been taken to the zoo by our neighbours.

items.sentence: The online lesson included a short sentence about The passive (2): the missing part is ...... .
items.answer: Kyle might be invited to the party.

items.sentence: During the school club on The passive (2), Nina wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: The party can be held at Jack's house.
